title: Vectorize hot loops with SIMD
description: Express data-parallel inner loops so the compiler auto-vectorizes, or use intrinsics (SSE/AVX/NEON) when it cannot, after confirming the loop is compute-bound.
body: Use when a loop is compute-bound, data-parallel, and already cache-friendly. Do not vectorize a memory-bound loop (you will saturate bandwidth, not gain) and do not assume the compiler vectorized it. Actions: remove loop-carried dependencies and aliasing (restrict), align data, enable the target ISA, then inspect the emitted assembly for packed instructions; fall back to intrinsics only where auto-vectorization fails. Validation signals include vectorized opcodes in the disassembly and measured speedup matching the lane width on representative input. Risk boundary: ISA-specific intrinsics break portability and need runtime feature detection; escalate before shipping AVX-512 paths without a scalar fallback.
